checks.a_analysis: The text present in the heading and the primary contact paragraph of the JSON faithfully represents the source. The AI-generated title in metadata and alt text for images are permitted additions per the instructions and do not constitute fabrication.

The mailing address and phone number provided in the output match the source exactly. No fabricated contact details (incorrect values) were found. While an email is omitted from the contact block, there are no instances of invented contact data.

The single page of the source document is represented in the output. Although the text within the flowchart is not transcribed into text elements, the flowchart itself is present as an image element, meaning the section is structurally present in the conversion.
The source document does not contain any tables.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output fails to transcribe the text content of the main flowchart, which contains the majority of the informative text in the document. All 7 steps, sub-points, and conditional logic (Yes/No paths) are absent as text from the JSON elements. This represents a loss of far more than 25% of the document's total text content.
checks.b_evidence_source: Text within the flowchart diagram describing the 7-step onboarding process, including: 'Step 1 Kickoff Event...', 'Step 2 Execution of License and Usage Agreement', sub-points 1-5 under Step 2 (e.g., 'Identifies & provides Signatory Authority'), the 'Yes/No' decision logic, and instructions for each path.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text from the flowchart is entirely omitted from the 'elements' array; it is only partially summarized in AI-generated alt text which is not a substitute for semantic text transcription.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The email address 'cjleadshelp@nc.gov' from the 'Primary Contact' section is missing in the output. This is a wording change (omission) that results in a user receiving less information than is present in the source.
checks.b_evidence_output: Primary Contact: CJLEADS 3514 Bush Street Raleigh, NC 27609 919-754-6949

checks.a_analysis: The output contains no duplicated text blocks. Each major section of the source document is represented once in the output structure, with the flowchart content summarized in the second image's alt text.

The output uses a single H1 heading for the document title. There are no other headings in the source document to establish a deeper hierarchy; therefore, the hierarchy is valid and consistent with the source.
There are no tables in the source document. The flowchart is a graphical process map and is correctly not interpreted as a table.

The reading order in the output is significantly corrupted. Multiple structural text blocks are entirely missing from the element list, including the 'Step 1' box ('Kickoff Event with CJLEADS Team...') and the 'CJLEADS' footer label. Furthermore, the 'reading_order' indices in the JSON array jump from 2 to 5, indicating that elements 3 and 4 are missing or were dropped during conversion. The contact information block (reading_order 2) is placed before the logo (reading_order 5), which is a deviation from the top-down flow of the original document.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source contains a box labeled 'Step 1' stating 'Kickoff Event with CJLEADS Team will provide overview of on-boarding process' and a footer label 'CJLEADS'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON output completely omits the text for Step 1 and the footer label. Reading order indices for elements skip from 2 to 5.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout document, element indices 3 and 4 are missing.

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains several numbered lists within the flowchart steps (e.g., Step 2 has 5 items, Step 5 has 2 items). The output does not contain any list-type elements. All structural list data has been lost and is only partially reflected in the alt text of the flowchart image, failing to represent the document's structure properly.
checks.b_evidence_source: Requesting Agency: 1. Identifies & provides Signatory Authority 2. Identifies internal points of contact...
checks.b_evidence_output: The output contains no 'list' type elements.
checks.b_evidence_location: Within the flowchart section of the source.

checks.a_analysis: The email address 'cjleadshelp@nc.gov' present in the contact information block of the source PDF is entirely missing from the output paragraph. The phone number is present but not structured as a clickable link.
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "Primary Contact: CJLEADS 3514 Bush Street Raleigh, NC 27609 919-754-6949"

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully represents the visual organization of the source PDF. The title and contact information, which are positioned around the flowchart in the source, are presented as text elements at the top of the HTML page, followed by the flowchart itself as a high-fidelity image. This sequential rendering preserves the visual flow and ensures the user can find the same information in a logical order. The conversion from a fixed-page layout to a web-based single-column layout is performed correctly without confusing the meaning of the document.

The source document does not contain any traditional tables. It consists of a flowchart with interconnected boxes and a contact information block. These elements are rendered as a single image in the HTML, maintaining their visual integrity and readability. There are no misrendered tables in the output.

All meaningful images from the source PDF, including the CJLEADS logo and the comprehensive flowchart diagram, are present in the HTML output. The flowchart image in the HTML is clear and contains all the visual components (boxes, arrows, color-coding) found in the original document. No meaningful images are missing or broken.

While the title 'How Does My Agency Get CJLEADS?' and the primary contact information appear both as HTML text and within the flowchart image, this is not a defect. According to the instructions, titles and headings appearing in both navigation/header areas and the body content are expected accessible design features. The repetition of the contact info at the top serves as a document header, which is standard for accessible conversion. There is no genuine body content (like paragraphs or tables) that is erroneously duplicated.
